title: Lithospermum cyrousianum
intro: Lithospermum cyrtusianum, also known as Lithospermum, is a perennial plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae), characterized by its blue or purple flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including inflammation, pain, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lithospermum include lithospermic acid, which is believed to be responsible for its medicinal effects, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Lithospermum is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also incorporated into creams and ointments for topical application.
taxonomy: Lithospermum cyrousianum is classified within the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family. This family consists of about 140 genera and 2,000 species of flowering plants. As a member of this family, Lithospermum cyrousianum is a flowering plant native to Iran. The Boraginaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Lithospermum being part of the Boraginoideae subfamily. The genus Lithospermum is a small group of flowering plants, consisting of about 20 species.
